skill.name: vercel-react-best-practices
skill.description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. Provides 45 rules across 8 categories for eliminating waterfalls, optimizing bundles, and improving rendering performance.
skill.summary: Comprehensive React and Next.js performance optimization guide with 45 prioritized rules for AI-assisted code generation and refactoring.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only containing React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines. All 999 static analyzer findings are false positives from code examples in markdown documentation files. The skill does not execute code, make network requests, or access sensitive data. It provides educational content and code patterns for AI to reference when writing or reviewing React/Next.js code.

content.user_title: Optimize React and Next.js Performance with Vercel Guidelines
content.value_statement: Building performant React applications requires understanding complex optimization patterns. This skill provides 45 prioritized rules from Vercel Engineering to guide AI in writing, reviewing, and refactoring React and Next.js code for optimal performance.
actual_capabilities: Provides 45 prioritized performance rules across 8 categories from critical to incremental impact
Guides elimination of async waterfalls that cause sequential network latency
Recommends bundle optimization techniques including direct imports and dynamic loading
Advises on server-side caching strategies with React.cache() and LRU patterns
Suggests re-render optimization using memo, useMemo, and functional setState
Provides code examples showing incorrect versus correct implementations for each rule
limitations: This skill provides documentation and guidelines only - it does not execute or test code
Rules are optimized for React 18+ and Next.js 13+ App Router patterns
Performance metrics are estimates and may vary based on application complexity
Some advanced patterns require understanding of React concurrency features

use_cases.title: Code Review and Refactoring
use_cases.description: Review existing React components for performance anti-patterns and suggest improvements based on Vercel guidelines

use_cases.title: New Component Development
use_cases.description: Generate new React and Next.js components following performance best practices from the start

use_cases.title: Performance Audit
use_cases.description: Analyze application codebase for waterfall patterns, bundle bloat, and rendering inefficiencies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this React component for performance issues using Vercel best practices. Identify any waterfalls, unnecessary re-renders, or bundle optimization opportunities:

[Paste component code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick code review of a single component

prompt_templates.title: Data Fetching Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt:
I have these API calls that are running sequentially. Refactor them using Vercel's waterfall elimination patterns to run in parallel where possible:

[Paste data fetching code]

prompt_templates.title: Bundle Size Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze these imports for bundle optimization opportunities. Suggest direct imports, dynamic imports, or preload strategies based on Vercel guidelines:

[Paste import statements and component usage]
prompt_templates.scenario: Reducing bundle size and improving load times

prompt_templates.title: Full Component Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt:
Refactor this Next.js page component following all applicable Vercel React best practices. Address waterfalls, caching, re-renders, and bundle optimization. Explain each change made:

[Paste full page component]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive performance optimization of a page

output_examples.input: Review this component that fetches user data and posts sequentially
output: Issue: Waterfall pattern detected - posts fetch waits for user fetch to complete
Fix: Use Promise.all() to fetch both resources in parallel
Expected improvement: 50-100ms reduction in load time (eliminates one network round trip)

output_examples.input: Optimize these icon imports from lucide-react
output: Current: Importing entire lucide-react library (1,583 modules, ~2.8s dev boot)
Recommended: Use direct imports from dist/esm/icons/ for each icon
Expected improvement: 15-70% faster dev boot, 40% faster cold starts

best_practices: Always start with waterfall elimination - it provides the highest impact performance gains
Use Promise.all() for independent async operations and defer await until results are needed
Import directly from package subpaths instead of barrel files to reduce bundle size
anti_patterns: Sequential await statements for independent operations causes unnecessary latency
Importing entire component libraries when only a few exports are needed
Using array sort() for finding min/max values instead of a single loop iteration

faq.question: What React and Next.js versions are these guidelines optimized for?
faq.answer: These guidelines target React 18+ with concurrent features and Next.js 13+ App Router with Server Components. Some patterns may apply to earlier versions but full benefits require modern React features.

faq.question: How do I prioritize which rules to apply first?
faq.answer: Start with CRITICAL category rules (eliminating waterfalls, bundle optimization) as they provide the largest performance gains. Then address HIGH and MEDIUM categories based on your application's specific bottlenecks.

faq.question: Do these guidelines apply to client-side only React applications?
faq.answer: Most guidelines apply to any React application. Server-specific rules (server-cache-react, server-serialization) only apply to Next.js App Router with Server Components.

faq.question: What is the expected performance impact of following these guidelines?
faq.answer: Impact varies by application. Waterfall elimination can reduce page load by 200-500ms. Bundle optimization can improve initial load by 15-40%. Re-render optimizations typically improve interaction responsiveness by 10-30%.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code for automated refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is designed for AI assistants. Provide the skill context and ask Claude Code to review or refactor code using the Vercel best practices guidelines.

faq.question: Are there any trade-offs to following these patterns?
faq.answer: Some patterns add code complexity (e.g., manual parallelization, direct imports). The performance benefits typically outweigh complexity costs, but apply judgment based on your team's needs and application scale.
